title: Festivals & events
subtitle: 43 celebrations through the year — pick a day to see what's on.

festivals.name: Neujahrskonzert der Berliner Philharmoniker
festivals.description: The Berlin Philharmonic's prestigious New Year's concert, broadcast worldwide and featuring classical masterworks. A cornerstone of German cultural tradition since 1882.

festivals.name: Neujahrstag
festivals.description: New Year's Day, a nationwide public holiday celebrating the start of the new year. Banks, shops and most businesses are closed.

festivals.name: International Filmfestspiele Berlin
festivals.description: One of the world's most prestigious film festivals showcasing international cinema and hosting major red-carpet premieres. A highlight of the global film calendar.

festivals.name: Fasching
festivals.description: Bavaria's spectacular carnival season with elaborate parades, costumes and street celebrations. Known for its distinctly Bavarian twist on traditional carnival festivities.

festivals.name: Cologne Carnival
festivals.description: The Rhineland's most exuberant carnival with three days of street parades, music and traditional costumes. Famous for its satirical floats and 'Drei Jecken' celebration.

festivals.name: Düsseldorf Carnival
festivals.description: A vibrant Rhineland carnival with elaborate processions and traditional Düsseldorf humor. Known for its unique blend of music, dance and satirical performances.

festivals.name: Mainz Carnival
festivals.description: The Rhineland's oldest carnival with traditional processions and political satire. A centuries-old celebration of Mainz's unique carnival culture.

festivals.name: Karfreitag
festivals.description: Good Friday, a nationwide public holiday observed two days before Easter Sunday. Most businesses close for this significant Christian holiday.

festivals.name: Ostersonntag
festivals.description: Easter Sunday, celebrated across Germany with family gatherings, Easter markets and egg hunts. A major nationwide religious holiday.

festivals.name: Ostermontag
festivals.description: Easter Monday, the day after Easter Sunday and a nationwide public holiday. Businesses and schools remain closed.

festivals.name: Wagner Festival
festivals.description: The world's oldest music festival dedicated exclusively to Richard Wagner's operas. Held in the specially designed Festspielhaus with performances of the complete Ring cycle.

festivals.name: Oktoberfest
festivals.description: The world's largest beer festival celebrating Bavarian culture with traditional beer halls, food and folk music. A global phenomenon attracting millions annually.

festivals.name: Tag der Deutschen Einheit
festivals.description: German Unity Day, celebrating the reunification of Germany on October 3, 1990. A nationwide public holiday marked by patriotic celebrations and ceremonies.

festivals.name: Leipzig Book Fair
festivals.description: Germany's oldest book fair and one of Europe's largest literary events showcasing publishers, authors and readers. A cornerstone of German literary culture.

festivals.name: Frankfurt Book Fair
festivals.description: The world's largest international book fair attracting publishers and authors from around the globe. A major event in the global publishing calendar.

festivals.name: Documenta
festivals.description: A legendary contemporary art exhibition held every five years, showcasing cutting-edge modern and contemporary art. One of the world's most influential art exhibitions.

festivals.name: Love Parade
festivals.description: A massive electronic dance music festival and techno parade celebrating music culture. An iconic Berlin event drawing hundreds of thousands of visitors.

festivals.name: Nuremberg Christkindlesmarkt
festivals.description: Germany's most famous Christmas market with over 180 wooden stalls selling crafts and traditional Nuremberg specialties. Dating back to the 16th century.

festivals.name: Cologne Christmas Markets
festivals.description: Multiple enchanting Christmas markets throughout Cologne featuring traditional crafts, mulled wine and festive atmosphere. The city's most magical season.

festivals.name: Berlin Christmas Markets
festivals.description: Numerous festive Christmas markets across Berlin offering holiday treats and traditional crafts. A quintessential Berlin winter experience.

festivals.name: Hamburg Port Anniversary
festivals.description: A three-day celebration of Hamburg's historic port with historical ship parades, cultural performances and maritime festivities. One of Germany's oldest harbor celebrations.

festivals.name: Kiel Week
festivals.description: The world's largest sailing festival featuring international yacht races and maritime celebrations. A premier event for sailing enthusiasts globally.

festivals.name: Wacken Open Air
festivals.description: Europe's largest open-air heavy metal festival attracting over 75,000 metalheads from around the world. A pilgrimage site for metal music fans.

festivals.name: Rock am Ring
festivals.description: Germany's largest rock and pop music festival with multiple stages and international headliners. A major annual gathering for rock and alternative music fans.

festivals.name: Carnival of Cultures
festivals.description: Berlin's multicultural street festival celebrating diverse cultures with parades, music and food. A vibrant display of Berlin's cosmopolitan character.

festivals.name: Deutschlandfest
festivals.description: A nationwide celebration of German Unity Day featuring outdoor festivals, cultural events and music across the country. Coincides with October 3rd festivities.

festivals.name: Heidelberg Theater Festival
festivals.description: A prestigious international theater festival in romantic Heidelberg showcasing classical and contemporary performances. Held in atmospheric historic venues.

festivals.name: Asparagus Festival
festivals.description: A culinary celebration of white asparagus harvest season with tastings, cooking demonstrations and regional dishes. A spring tradition in Germany's asparagus country.

festivals.name: Dresdner Musikfestspielen
festivals.description: Dresden's prestigious classical music festival featuring world-class orchestras and soloists in beautiful historic venues. A major event in European classical music calendar.

festivals.name: Weimar Classical Open
festivals.description: An international classical music festival celebrating Weimar's rich cultural heritage and connections to Goethe and Schiller. Performances held in historic venues.

festivals.name: Berlin Marathon
festivals.description: The world's largest marathon attracting over 40,000 runners with a route through Berlin's historic neighborhoods. A global sporting spectacle.

festivals.name: Pfingstsonntag
festivals.description: Whit Sunday, the seventh Sunday after Easter, is a nationwide public holiday. Marks the descent of the Holy Spirit in Christian tradition.

festivals.name: Pfingstmontag
festivals.description: Whit Monday, the day after Whit Sunday, is a nationwide public holiday. Businesses and schools remain closed.

festivals.name: Ascension Day
festivals.description: Ascension Day, celebrated 39 days after Easter, is a nationwide public holiday marking Jesus's ascension. A major Christian observance across Germany.

festivals.name: Tag der Arbeit
festivals.description: Labor Day, a nationwide public holiday celebrating workers and labor movements. International Workers' Day with various demonstrations and festivities.

festivals.name: Karneval der Kulturen
festivals.description: A multicultural celebration showcasing Berlin's diverse communities through parades, music and food. One of Berlin's most colorful festivals.

festivals.name: Jena Light Festival
festivals.description: An innovative light art festival transforming Jena's streets with installations and projections. A unique celebration of contemporary light and art.

festivals.name: Bregenzer Festspiele Overflow
festivals.description: Cultural events associated with Austria's Bregenz Festival activities near the German-Austrian border. A major regional musical and theatrical celebration.

festivals.name: Bremer Freimarkt
festivals.description: One of Germany's oldest fairs dating back to 1035 with traditional rides, food and carnival atmosphere. A centuries-old autumn celebration.

festivals.name: Hanover Industrial Fair
festivals.description: The world's largest industrial technology trade fair showcasing innovations and cutting-edge technology. A premier international business and technology event.

festivals.name: Allerheiligen
festivals.description: All Saints' Day is a public holiday in certain German regions, particularly Catholic areas. Traditionally observed with cemetery visits and commemorations.

festivals.name: Fronleichnam
festivals.description: Corpus Christi, celebrated 60 days after Easter, is a public holiday in Catholic regions. Marked by elaborate processions and religious ceremonies.

festivals.name: Silvester
festivals.description: New Year's Eve with fireworks, parties and celebrations across Germany. A nationwide festive night welcoming the new year.
